_meta.description: Case lifecycle, resolution, merging, routing rules, and case configuration.

mcq.q: Which Dynamics 365 Customer Service entity is the primary record for tracking a customer's issue or request?
options: Activity
Case
Queue Item
Entitlement
mcq.explanation: Cases (also called Incidents) are the central entity in Dynamics 365 Customer Service. All customer issues, service requests, and support interactions are tracked as cases.

wrongExplanations.0: Activities (calls, emails, tasks) are child records linked to a case — they are not the primary tracking record.
wrongExplanations.2: This option is incorrect — see the main explanation.
wrongExplanations.3: Queue Items are wrappers that place records in queues for routing — they reference a case but are not the case itself.

mcq.q: A customer calls in and an agent creates a case directly from the phone call activity. Which feature enables this workflow?
options: Entitlement Channel
Convert to Case
Case Creation Rule
Unified Routing
mcq.explanation: The Convert to Case action on Activity records allows agents to create a case directly from a phone call, email, or other activity, linking the activity to the new case automatically.

wrongExplanations.0: Entitlement Channels define which contact channels (phone, email, web) decrement against an entitlement — not case creation from activities.
wrongExplanations.2: This option is incorrect — see the main explanation.
wrongExplanations.3: Case Creation Rules automatically create cases from inbound emails — agents use Convert to Case for manual activity conversion.

mcq.q: When an agent resolves a case in Dynamics 365 Customer Service, which activity record is automatically created to capture the resolution details?
options: Task
Case Resolution Activity
Appointment
Note
mcq.explanation: Resolving a case creates a Case Resolution Activity record that stores the resolution type, billable and total time, and the resolution description. This activity is linked to the case for audit and reporting.

wrongExplanations.0: Tasks are manual activities created by agents — they are not created automatically on case resolution.
wrongExplanations.2: This option is incorrect — see the main explanation.
wrongExplanations.3: Appointments are calendar-based activities — not created on case resolution.

mcq.q: Which case status reason is set when a case is resolved in Dynamics 365 Customer Service?
options: Closed
Resolved
Problem Solved
Completed
mcq.explanation: When a case is resolved, its Status Reason is set to Problem Solved (the default resolution reason). Administrators can add custom resolution reasons, but Problem Solved is the system default.

wrongExplanations.0: Closed is a status, not a status reason. The Status becomes Resolved when a case is resolved.
wrongExplanations.1: Resolved is the case Status (not Status Reason) that is set when the case is resolved.
wrongExplanations.3: This option is incorrect — see the main explanation.

mcq.q: A company wants agents to confirm that they have communicated the resolution to the customer before closing a case. Which configuration achieves this?
options: Add a mandatory field to the Case Resolution dialog
Create a Business Rule that enforces the field before allowing status change
Use a Business Process Flow stage that requires a Customer Notified step before moving to Resolved
Configure an SLA action to block resolution until the customer responds
mcq.explanation: Business Process Flows (BPFs) guide agents through required stages. By adding a Customer Notified step with a required field in the stage preceding resolution, agents cannot advance to the Resolved stage without completing that step.

wrongExplanations.0: The Case Resolution dialog has limited customisation — adding mandatory fields there is not straightforward.
wrongExplanations.1: A Business Rule can enforce field requirements on a form but does not block the resolution dialog action itself.
wrongExplanations.3: This option is incorrect — see the main explanation.

mcq.q: Which setting controls whether a resolved case can be reactivated by agents in Dynamics 365 Customer Service?
options: Case Settings in Customer Service Hub admin
Service Configuration Settings — specifically the Allow resolved cases to be reactivated option
Security role privileges on the Case entity
Business Rule on the Case form
mcq.explanation: The Service Configuration Settings in the Customer Service admin centre include an option to allow or disallow reactivation of resolved cases. When disabled, agents cannot reopen resolved cases and must create a new case instead.

wrongExplanations.0: Customer Service Hub admin is where this is found, but the specific setting is within Service Configuration Settings.
wrongExplanations.2: This option is incorrect — see the main explanation.
wrongExplanations.3: Security roles control who can perform actions but do not toggle the system-level reactivation capability.

mcq.q: An agent cancels a case rather than resolving it. Which status reason is applied by default?
options: Cancelled
Duplicate
Merged
Withdrawn
mcq.explanation: When an agent uses the Cancel Case action, the Status becomes Cancelled and the default Status Reason is Duplicate. Administrators can add additional cancellation reasons to accommodate different cancellation scenarios.

wrongExplanations.0: Cancelled is the Status, not the Status Reason, when a case is cancelled.
wrongExplanations.2: This option is incorrect — see the main explanation.
wrongExplanations.3: Merged is a separate status reason used when two cases are merged together — not the default cancellation reason.

mcq.q: Which feature allows a supervisor to combine two related cases into a single primary case and automatically close the secondary one?
options: Case Routing
Case Merging
Case Linking
Case Escalation
mcq.explanation: Case Merging combines two case records into one. The secondary case is cancelled with a Merged status reason, and all activities, notes, and child records from both cases are associated with the primary case.

wrongExplanations.0: Case Routing assigns cases to queues or agents — it does not combine cases.
wrongExplanations.2: This option is incorrect — see the main explanation.
wrongExplanations.3: Case Linking associates related cases together for reference but keeps both cases active — it does not close either one.

mcq.q: When configuring the Case Resolution dialog box, which admin setting allows you to require agents to provide a resolution description before closing a case?
options: Make resolution description required in the Case entity field settings
Enable Require description in resolution dialog in Service Configuration Settings
Add a business rule on the Case entity for the resolution field
Customise the Case Resolution Activity form to mark the field as required
mcq.explanation: The Service Configuration Settings in Customer Service admin include a toggle to require a resolution description in the resolution dialog. When enabled, agents cannot resolve a case without entering a description.

wrongExplanations.0: The Case entity field settings do not control the resolution dialog required field behaviour.
wrongExplanations.2: This option is incorrect — see the main explanation.
wrongExplanations.3: Business Rules operate on form logic — they do not intercept the resolution dialog popup.

mcq.q: The Case table in D365 Customer Service has three possible values for the Status field. What are they?
options: Open, In Progress, Closed
Active, Resolved, Canceled
New, Active, Closed
Draft, Active, Resolved
mcq.explanation: The Case (Incident) table has exactly three Status values: Active, Resolved, and Canceled. The Status Reason field provides more granular sub-states within each of these. Active status reasons include In Progress, On Hold, Waiting for Details, and Researching.

wrongExplanations.0: Open, In Progress, Closed are not the documented Status field values for the Case table.
wrongExplanations.2: New is not a standard Case status value — the three values are Active, Resolved, and Canceled.
wrongExplanations.3: Draft is not a standard Case status value.

mcq.q: Which of the following is a valid Status Reason for a case with Active status in D365 Customer Service (out-of-box)?
options: Merged
Problem Solved
Waiting for Details
Information Provided
mcq.explanation: The out-of-box Active status reasons are: In Progress, On Hold, Waiting for Details, and Researching. 'Waiting for Details' is used when an agent is waiting for additional information from the customer before progressing the case.

wrongExplanations.0: Merged is a Canceled status reason — it indicates the case was merged into another.
wrongExplanations.1: Problem Solved is a Resolved status reason, not an Active one.
wrongExplanations.3: Information Provided is a Resolved status reason.

mcq.q: A case was merged into another case. Which Status and Status Reason combination will the merged case have?
options: Active — Merged
Resolved — Merged
Canceled — Merged
Closed — Duplicate
mcq.explanation: When a case is merged into another, its status is set to Canceled with the Status Reason of Merged. The case is effectively closed and cannot be re-opened. All activities from the merged case are moved to the primary case.

wrongExplanations.0: Merged is a Canceled status reason — the status cannot be Active when a case is merged.
wrongExplanations.1: Resolved is for cases that have been fixed — merging uses Canceled, not Resolved.
wrongExplanations.3: 'Duplicate' is not an out-of-box status reason for merged cases — Merged is the correct reason.

mcq.q: An administrator wants to restrict which Status Reason values an agent can transition to from 'On Hold'. Which feature is used to configure this?
options: Business Rules on the Case form
Status Reason Transitions defined in Power Apps
Case Resolution Dialog configuration
Workflow step on status change
mcq.explanation: Status Reason Transitions define which status reason values a case can move to from a given status reason. This is configured via the Status Reason column properties in Power Apps (legacy experience). This prevents agents from selecting illogical status reason combinations — for example, jumping directly from On Hold to a Resolved reason.

wrongExplanations.0: Business Rules on the Case form can show/hide or lock fields, but they don't restrict the Status Reason dropdown options via the transition model.
wrongExplanations.2: The Case Resolution Dialog controls what fields appear when resolving — not which status reasons are reachable.
wrongExplanations.3: A workflow on status change fires after the transition — it does not prevent invalid transitions.

mcq.q: What are the out-of-box Status Reason values for a Resolved case in D365 Customer Service?
options: Completed, Closed, Fixed
Problem Solved, Information Provided
Done, Resolved, Handled
Merged, Duplicate, Resolved
mcq.explanation: The out-of-box Resolved status reasons are Problem Solved and Information Provided. Problem Solved is used when the technical issue was fixed; Information Provided is used when the customer just needed guidance or information rather than a fix.

wrongExplanations.0: Completed, Closed, and Fixed are not out-of-box Status Reason values on the Case table.
wrongExplanations.2: Done, Resolved, and Handled are not out-of-box Status Reason values.
wrongExplanations.3: Merged and Duplicate are Canceled status reasons — not Resolved ones.

mcq.q: A custom Status Reason 'Duplicate' needs to be added to the case resolution workflow. In which TWO tables must the custom value be added and kept in sync?
options: Case table and Case Activity table
Case table and Case Resolution table
Case table and Incident Resolution table
Status Reason table and Case Resolution table
mcq.explanation: When adding a custom Status Reason to the customizable case resolution dialog, the value must be added to the Status Reason column on the Case table AND the Resolution Type column on the Case Resolution table — and the numeric values must match exactly. If values don't match, the custom option won't appear in the dialog and an error may occur.

wrongExplanations.0: There is no 'Case Activity' table in this context — the second table is Case Resolution.
wrongExplanations.2: The table is called Case Resolution (msdyn_caseresolution) — not Incident Resolution.
wrongExplanations.3: There is no standalone 'Status Reason table' — the column lives on the Case and Case Resolution tables.

mcq.q: Where does an administrator configure parent and child case settings in D365 Customer Service?
options: Settings > Service Management > Service Configuration
Copilot Service admin center > Customer Support > Case Settings > Manage > Parent child case settings
Power Apps > Tables > Case > Relationships
Settings > Advanced Settings > Case Hierarchy
mcq.explanation: Parent and child case settings are configured in Copilot Service admin center under Customer Support > Case Settings > Manage (for Parent child case settings). The settings include which attributes child cases inherit from the parent, the closure preference, and maximum child case count.

wrongExplanations.0: Service Management > Service Configuration is the legacy path — the current admin path is Copilot Service admin center.
wrongExplanations.2: Power Apps > Tables handles data model relationships, but the business behaviour settings for parent-child cases are in the admin center.
wrongExplanations.3: There is no 'Case Hierarchy' option in Advanced Settings.

mcq.q: Which two case attributes are MANDATORY when configuring what child cases inherit from their parent in D365 Customer Service?
options: Priority and Owner
Case Title and Customer
Case Origin and Subject
SLA and Entitlement
mcq.explanation: Case Title and Customer are the two mandatory inherited attributes in the parent-child case settings dialog. These cannot be removed from the Selected list. Additional attributes can be added by moving them from the Available list to the Selected list.

wrongExplanations.0: Priority and Owner are not the mandatory inherited fields — they can be optionally selected.
wrongExplanations.2: Case Origin and Subject are optional — not mandatory.
wrongExplanations.3: SLA and Entitlement are not mandatory inherited attributes in the parent-child settings.

mcq.q: An organisation needs to prevent an agent from closing a parent case until all child cases are resolved. Which parent-child setting achieves this?
options: Close all child cases when parent case is closed
Don't allow parent case closure until all child cases are closed
Maximum number of child cases = 0
Enable Business Rule: Block parent if children exist
mcq.explanation: The 'Don't allow parent case closure until all child cases are closed' setting in the parent-child case settings dialog prevents agents from resolving or canceling a parent case while any child cases remain open. This enforces complete resolution at all levels.

wrongExplanations.0: 'Close all child cases when parent case is closed' does the opposite — it cascades the closure downward, not blocks the parent from closing.
wrongExplanations.2: Maximum child cases = 0 would prevent any child cases from being created at all.
wrongExplanations.3: There is no built-in Business Rule for this — the closure preference setting achieves this in the admin center.

mcq.q: Why does Microsoft recommend specifying a smaller value for Maximum number of child cases?
options: Licensing is calculated per child case — more children increase cost
To avoid performance issues when cases are resolved or merged
Child case limits are enforced by Dataverse row capacity
Larger child counts cause the parent case form to load slowly in all browsers
mcq.explanation: Microsoft's documentation explicitly recommends specifying a smaller value for Maximum number of child cases to avoid performance issues when cases are resolved or merged. Processing large numbers of related child cases in a single operation can cause timeouts or slow down the system.

wrongExplanations.0: Child case count does not affect licensing in D365 Customer Service.
wrongExplanations.2: While Dataverse has storage limits, the recommendation is specifically about performance, not row capacity.
wrongExplanations.3: The concern is about server-side processing during resolve/merge operations — not browser-side form loading.

mcq.q: An agent creates a child case from a parent case. Which fields will be automatically populated on the child case based on the default mandatory inherited attributes?
options: Owner and Priority from the parent
Case Title and Customer from the parent
Subject and Case Origin from the parent
SLA and Entitlement from the parent
mcq.explanation: By default, Case Title and Customer are the mandatory attributes that child cases inherit from the parent. The child case's Title and Customer fields will be pre-populated from the parent. Administrators can configure additional attributes to inherit in the parent-child case settings.

wrongExplanations.0: Owner and Priority are not the mandatory inherited attributes — they are optional additions.
wrongExplanations.2: Subject and Case Origin are not mandatory inherited fields.
wrongExplanations.3: SLA and Entitlement inheritance is configurable but not mandatory by default.

mcq.q: A company tracks warranty claims for products. Each claim has sub-issues (electrical fault, mechanical fault, cosmetic issue). Using parent-child case settings, which closure option should they use to ensure all sub-issues are addressed before closing the main claim?
options: Close all child cases when parent case is closed — to batch close everything at once
Don't allow parent case closure until all child cases are closed — to enforce complete sub-issue resolution
Maximum number of child cases = 1 — to prevent splitting claims
Leave default — parent and child can be closed independently
mcq.explanation: For warranty claims where all sub-issues must be addressed, the 'Don't allow parent case closure until all child cases are closed' setting enforces this. Without it, an agent could close the main claim while sub-issues remain open. This is the correct business rule for requiring all child work to complete before the parent is done.

wrongExplanations.0: 'Close all child cases when parent is closed' would cascade-close sub-issues regardless of whether they were actually resolved — not appropriate for warranty tracking.
wrongExplanations.2: Limiting to 1 child case would prevent the multiple sub-issue tracking the scenario requires.
wrongExplanations.3: Leaving the default means no enforcement — the parent could be closed while sub-issues remain open.

mcq.q: What is the fundamental difference between Visual Filters and Global Filters on a D365 Customer Service interactive dashboard?
options: Visual Filters apply to streams only; Global Filters apply to charts only
Visual Filters are interactive charts that filter streams when clicked; Global Filters apply text/field criteria to all streams and charts simultaneously
Visual Filters are set by the administrator; Global Filters can be set by individual users
Visual Filters persist between sessions; Global Filters are cleared when the page refreshes
mcq.explanation: Visual Filters are interactive charts (pie, bar, doughnut, tag) displayed above the dashboard streams. Clicking a segment — e.g. the 'High Priority' slice — filters all streams to show only records matching that criterion. Global Filters are flyout-panel field criteria (e.g. filter by Escalated=Yes or Case Type=Request) that apply to ALL streams and charts simultaneously.

wrongExplanations.0: Both filter types affect streams — Visual Filters also update streams, not just charts.
wrongExplanations.2: Both filter types are available to users on the dashboard — Visual Filter charts are configured by admins but used by anyone.
wrongExplanations.3: Both Visual and Global filters are cleared when removed by the user or when the page is refreshed — neither persists.

mcq.q: A manager needs to see only escalated cases in the Tier 1 dashboard. Which filter type should they use?
options: Visual Filter — click the Escalated chart segment
Global Filter — apply Escalated=Yes field filter
Timeframe Filter — set to Today's escalated cases
Stream sort — sort by Escalated field descending
mcq.explanation: The Global Filter is used to apply field-level criteria across the entire dashboard — streams, charts, and tiles. To show only escalated cases, the manager opens the Global Filter flyout and selects Escalated=Yes, then applies it. This refreshes all dashboard components to show only matching records.

wrongExplanations.0: Visual Filter requires an 'Escalated' chart to be present on the dashboard and only filters streams — the Global Filter is the correct tool for field-criteria filtering.
wrongExplanations.2: Timeframe Filter controls the date range of records displayed — it does not filter by field values like escalation status.
wrongExplanations.3: Stream sorting changes the order of records but does not hide non-escalated cases.

mcq.q: On a multi-stream dashboard, an agent wants to display cases organised by Priority. What is the correct way to make the dashboard show cases by priority?
options: Enable a Visual Filter chart configured to Cases by Priority — the chart acts as a filter when clicked
Use the Global Filter to set Priority = High, Medium, Low separately
Change the stream sort order to Priority Ascending
Create a new stream with a Cases By Priority view
mcq.explanation: To display cases by priority on the dashboard, the Cases by Priority Visual Filter chart must be enabled. When an agent clicks the High Priority segment, the dashboard streams refresh to show only high priority cases. Visual Filter charts (configured per entity) are the primary tool for this type of at-a-glance priority view on interactive dashboards.

wrongExplanations.1: Global Filter applies field criteria — but using it for priority would require multiple separate filter applications, not a single visual split by priority.
wrongExplanations.2: Stream sort changes the order within a stream — it does not group or separate cases by priority across the dashboard.
wrongExplanations.3: Creating a separate stream per priority is possible but not the purpose-built solution — the Visual Filter chart is the intended tool.

mcq.q: Which dashboard type is most appropriate for a Tier 2 lead who monitors a small number of complex or escalated cases in a single queue?
options: Multi-stream dashboard
Single-stream dashboard
Entity-specific multi-stream dashboard
Tile-only dashboard
mcq.explanation: Single-stream dashboards display real-time data over one stream with tiles on the right side always shown. They are specifically designed for Tier 2 leads or managers who monitor fewer but more complex or escalated cases in a single view or queue, as opposed to multi-stream dashboards which suit Tier 1 agents handling many cases.

wrongExplanations.0: Multi-stream dashboards are optimised for Tier 1 support agents handling many cases simultaneously across multiple streams.
wrongExplanations.2: Entity-specific multi-stream dashboards (e.g. Cases dashboard) still show multiple streams and are best for Tier 1 or general case viewing.
wrongExplanations.3: There is no 'Tile-only dashboard' type — tiles are part of multi-stream and single-stream dashboards.

mcq.q: An agent is viewing an interactive dashboard with Visual Filters enabled. They click the 'High' segment on the Cases by Priority chart. Then they also apply a Global Filter for Escalated=Yes. What records will the dashboard show?
options: All high priority cases OR all escalated cases (either condition)
Only high priority escalated cases (both conditions applied simultaneously)
Only the Global Filter applies — clicking a Visual Filter segment is ignored when a Global Filter is active
Only the Visual Filter applies — Global Filters are overridden by chart selections
mcq.explanation: Visual Filters and Global Filters can be combined. When both are active, they work as AND conditions — the dashboard shows records that satisfy BOTH the Visual Filter selection (High priority) AND the Global Filter criteria (Escalated=Yes). This layered filtering helps drill down from a large list to a very specific subset.

wrongExplanations.0: The filters are AND conditions — not OR. Both must be satisfied.
wrongExplanations.2: Global Filters and Visual Filters work together — neither overrides the other.
wrongExplanations.3: Neither filter type overrides the other — they stack as AND conditions.

mcq.q: On a multi-stream interactive dashboard, how does an agent access the quick actions (resolve, cancel, assign) for a case directly from the dashboard stream without opening the case form?
options: Right-click the case row in the stream
Select the '...' (ellipsis/quick action menu) in the top right corner of the work item in the stream
Use Bulk Select and choose an action from the toolbar
Double-click the case to open a quick action flyout
mcq.explanation: Each work item in a dashboard stream has a '...' (ellipsis) quick action menu in its top right corner. Selecting it opens a quick action bar that includes Resolve, Cancel, and Assign. The available commands are configurable and shared between dashboards and grids — agents can action cases without leaving the dashboard.

wrongExplanations.0: Right-clicking does not open quick actions on dashboard stream items — the '...' menu is the correct mechanism.
wrongExplanations.2: Bulk Select allows selecting multiple records for batch actions — the '...' menu is for individual item quick actions.
wrongExplanations.3: Double-clicking opens the case form — not a flyout with quick actions.

mcq.q: What are the three case resolution dialog options available in D365 Customer Service (Copilot Service admin center)?
options: Legacy, Enhanced, Custom
Standard dialog, Customizable dialog, Quick create dialog
Minimal dialog, Full dialog, API-only dialog
Classic, Modern, Headless
mcq.explanation: In Copilot Service admin center > Case Settings > Other Settings, the Resolve case dialog dropdown offers three options: Standard dialog (the original minimal dialog), Customizable dialog (allows adding/removing fields via the Case Resolution form), and Quick create dialog. The choice determines what the agent sees when they click Resolve Case.

wrongExplanations.0: Legacy, Enhanced, Custom are not the documented options — Standard, Customizable, and Quick create are.
wrongExplanations.2: Minimal, Full, and API-only are not the documented options.
wrongExplanations.3: Classic, Modern, Headless are not the documented options for this setting.

mcq.q: An administrator selects 'Customizable dialog' for the case resolution dialog. How does an admin add the Billable Time field to this dialog?
options: Enable it in Case Settings > Other Settings > Billable Time toggle
In Power Apps > Tables > Case Resolution > Forms — add the Billable Time field to the form
In the Case form editor — drag Billable Time to the Resolution section
Modify the Case entity Status Reason column to include Billable Time
mcq.explanation: With Customizable dialog selected, the dialog is driven by the Case Resolution form in Power Apps. To add Billable Time, go to Power Apps > Tables > Case Resolution > Forms, open the required form, and add the Billable Time field. Note: The Total Time field must also be present for Billable Time to auto-calculate.

wrongExplanations.0: There is no Billable Time toggle in Case Settings — field customisation is done via Power Apps forms.
wrongExplanations.2: Billable Time is on the Case Resolution form (a separate table), not the Case form directly.
wrongExplanations.3: Status Reason is a Case column — Billable Time is a Case Resolution table column.

mcq.q: What must be true for the Billable Time field to display its auto-calculated value in the customizable case resolution dialog?
options: The SLA must be active on the case
The Total Time field must also be added to the case resolution dialog
Copilot Service workspace must be used — Customer Service Hub does not support Billable Time
The case must have at least one resolved activity before the dialog opens
mcq.explanation: Microsoft explicitly states: 'Ensure that the Total Time field is added to the case resolution dialog, for the Billable Time field to display the amount of time a representative worked on a case automatically.' Both fields must be present on the Case Resolution form for the auto-calculation to work.

wrongExplanations.0: SLA activity does not drive Billable Time calculation in the resolution dialog — Total Time does.
wrongExplanations.2: The Total Time requirement applies to both apps — it is not app-specific.
wrongExplanations.3: Activity resolution status does not drive Billable Time auto-population.

mcq.q: What is the difference between Case Handling Time and Case Resolution Time in D365 Customer Service?
options: They are the same — both measure total time from creation to resolution
Handling Time is the active working time spent on the case; Resolution Time is total elapsed time from creation to resolution
Handling Time is for Tier 1 cases; Resolution Time is for Tier 2 cases
Handling Time includes SLA breaches; Resolution Time excludes them
mcq.explanation: Case Handling Time is the total time representatives actively worked on the case — communicating, troubleshooting, updating notes. Case Resolution Time is the total elapsed time from case creation to resolution. For example: a rep works 5 active hours over 5 days. Handling Time = 5 hours. Resolution Time = 5 days. They measure very different things.

wrongExplanations.0: They measure different things — handling time is active work time; resolution time is calendar elapsed time.
wrongExplanations.2: The distinction is about what is measured, not case tier.
wrongExplanations.3: SLA breach tracking is a separate SLA KPI concept — neither time metric is defined this way.

mcq.q: The Case Handling Time widget is added to the case form but representatives say it is not tracking time automatically. In Customer Service Hub, what is the required condition for automatic time tracking?
options: Automatic time tracking works without the widget — time is tracked in the background
The widget must be added to the case form for automatic time tracking to work in Customer Service Hub
Automatic time tracking requires Copilot Studio to be enabled
The Experience Profile must have a 'Time Tracking' app tab configured
mcq.explanation: Microsoft distinguishes the two apps: In Copilot Service workspace, time is tracked automatically in the background even without the widget on the form. In Customer Service Hub, the widget MUST be added to the case form for automatic time tracking to occur. Without the widget on the form in Customer Service Hub, no time is tracked.

wrongExplanations.0: That is true for Copilot Service workspace only — in Customer Service Hub the widget must be present.
wrongExplanations.2: Copilot Studio (formerly Power Virtual Agents) is for bots — it has no role in case handling time tracking.
wrongExplanations.3: Experience Profiles configure app tabs and features — but the specific requirement here is the widget being on the form in CSHub.

mcq.q: By default, can an administrator update field values on a Resolved or Canceled case in D365 Customer Service?
options: No — resolved and canceled cases are permanently read-only
Yes — updates are allowed by default via Power Automate flows or API, without reopening the case
Yes — but only from the case form directly, not via API
No — to update a resolved case you must first reactivate it, make changes, then re-resolve
mcq.explanation: Allowing updates to resolved/canceled cases IS the default behaviour in D365 Customer Service. Updates must be performed via Power Automate flows or API — not via the case form UI. This is useful for GDPR data removal or audit corrections without reopening cases. The admin can restrict this in Case Settings > Other Settings > Case update after resolution.

wrongExplanations.0: Resolved/canceled cases are not permanently read-only — updates via Power Automate/API are allowed by default.
wrongExplanations.2: Updates are done via Power Automate or API — not directly from the case form UI.
wrongExplanations.3: Reactivating the case is one option, but it is not required — API/flow updates work without reactivation.

mcq.q: An organisation's legal team needs to redact personal data from resolved cases for GDPR compliance. The cases must remain in Resolved status. Which approach does D365 Customer Service support?
options: Reactivate each case, redact data, then re-resolve — mandatory workflow
Use Power Automate flows or API to update case records in resolved/canceled state without reopening
Use the Case form's GDPR Redact button available in the command bar
Resolved cases cannot be modified — data must be redacted at database level by a DBA
mcq.explanation: D365 Customer Service supports updating resolved and canceled cases via Power Automate flows or API without reactivating them. This is specifically documented as a GDPR compliance use case — allowing data to be removed or updated without changing the case's resolved status or history.

wrongExplanations.0: Reactivating is not mandatory — the platform supports direct flow/API updates on resolved cases.
wrongExplanations.2: There is no built-in GDPR Redact button in the Case form command bar — the mechanism is Power Automate or API.
wrongExplanations.3: Database-level manipulation is not the supported approach — Power Automate/API are the correct tools.

mcq.q: What setting controls whether agents can update resolved and/or canceled cases in D365 Customer Service?
options: Case Settings > Resolve case dialog > Allow updates toggle
Case Settings > Other Settings > Case update after resolution > Allow updates for dropdown
Settings > Service Configuration > Post-Resolution Editing
Security Role > Case entity > Write privilege scope
mcq.explanation: The setting is in Copilot Service admin center > Case Settings > Other Settings > Case update after resolution. The 'Allow updates for' dropdown provides options: Resolved and canceled cases, Resolved cases only, Closed cases only, or Don't allow updates. This controls update permissions at the application level.

wrongExplanations.0: The Resolve case dialog setting controls the dialog type — not post-resolution update permissions.
wrongExplanations.2: 'Service Configuration > Post-Resolution Editing' does not exist as a documented path.
wrongExplanations.3: Security Role privileges control who can write to case records — but the post-resolution update allowance is a separate application-level setting.

mcq.q: An agent resolves a case but realises they chose the wrong Status Reason. The case is now in Resolved status. What is the recommended approach to correct this in D365 Customer Service?
options: Delete the case and create a new one with the correct status reason
Reactivate the case, change the status reason to the correct value, then re-resolve
Use a Power Automate flow to update the Status Reason on the resolved case without reopening it
Both B and C are valid approaches depending on the organisation's policy
mcq.explanation: Both reactivating the case (then updating and re-resolving) and using Power Automate/API to update the resolved case directly are valid approaches. Reactivation is the UI-friendly path; Power Automate/API is the approach for keeping the case in resolved state throughout. The choice depends on organisational policy — both are supported by the platform.

wrongExplanations.0: Deleting and recreating a case is never the recommended approach — it destroys case history and related records.
wrongExplanations.1: Reactivating is valid, but so is Power Automate/API — making C also correct. The most accurate answer is D.
wrongExplanations.2: Power Automate/API is valid, but reactivation is also valid — making B also correct. The most accurate answer is D.

mcq.q: When an agent merges two cases in D365 Customer Service, what happens to the activities (emails, phone calls, notes) that were associated with the merged (secondary) case?
options: Activities are permanently deleted from the system
Activities are moved to the primary case and remain visible on its timeline
Activities remain on the secondary (merged) case but the case is locked
Activities are archived separately and can only be viewed from the merge history
mcq.explanation: When cases are merged, all activities (emails, phone calls, appointments, notes) from the secondary (merged) case are moved to the primary case. They become visible on the primary case's timeline. The secondary case gets status Canceled / Merged and its own form becomes read-only.

wrongExplanations.0: Activities are not deleted — they are moved to the primary case.
wrongExplanations.2: Activities do not stay on the secondary case — they are transferred to the primary case.
wrongExplanations.3: There is no 'merge history archive' — activities appear directly on the primary case timeline.

mcq.q: An agent has two duplicate cases for the same customer. Case A has 3 emails and an SLA KPI. Case B has 1 email. The agent merges Case B into Case A. What is the state of each case after the merge?
options: Case A: Active (unchanged). Case B: Resolved — Merged
Case A: Active with 4 emails total. Case B: Canceled — Merged
Both cases: Resolved — Merged
Case A: Closed. Case B: Canceled — Duplicate
mcq.explanation: After merging Case B into Case A: Case A (the primary) remains Active and now has all 4 emails (3 original + 1 from Case B). Case B (the secondary) is set to Canceled — Merged. The primary case retains its SLA KPI and all original data — the secondary case simply transfers its activities and becomes inactive.

wrongExplanations.0: The secondary case becomes Canceled — Merged (not Resolved — Merged).
wrongExplanations.2: Only the secondary case becomes Canceled — Merged. The primary stays Active.
wrongExplanations.3: Case A does not close — it remains Active. Canceled — Duplicate is not an out-of-box status reason.

mcq.q: Can an agent merge a case that is in Resolved or Canceled status as the primary case?
options: Yes — any case regardless of status can be the primary in a merge
No — only Active cases can be the primary case in a merge
Yes — but only if the Resolved case has no SLA KPI active
No — merging is only available from the dashboard, not from the case form
mcq.explanation: Only Active cases can be selected as the primary case in a merge. Resolved and Canceled cases cannot be used as the primary. This is a system constraint — the secondary (merged) case will become Canceled — Merged, so the primary must be in an active state to continue receiving work.

wrongExplanations.0: Status is a constraint — only Active cases can be the primary in a merge.
wrongExplanations.2: SLA KPI status does not affect merge eligibility — the Active status of the case is the constraint.
wrongExplanations.3: Merging is available from the case form command bar — not only from the dashboard.

mcq.q: What happens to the SLA KPI on the primary case when a merge occurs in D365 Customer Service?
options: The SLA KPI is reset to zero for the primary case
The SLA KPI on the primary case is unaffected — it continues as normal
The SLA KPI is recalculated based on the oldest creation date between the two cases
The SLA is deactivated on the primary case when a merge occurs
mcq.explanation: The SLA KPI on the primary case is not affected by the merge. It continues running against the primary case's original start time. The secondary case's SLA KPI (if any) is effectively abandoned when that case becomes Canceled — Merged.

wrongExplanations.0: The SLA KPI is not reset — it continues from its original start time.
wrongExplanations.2: The SLA continues from the primary case's own creation date — it is not recalculated based on the older case.
wrongExplanations.3: The SLA remains active on the primary case after a merge.

mcq.q: An agent accidentally merged Case 1 (primary) and Case 2 (secondary). Can the merge be undone in D365 Customer Service?
options: Yes — an Undo Merge button appears on the primary case for 24 hours after the merge
No — a merge cannot be undone. Case 2 remains in Canceled — Merged status permanently
Yes — System Administrators can reverse a merge from Settings > Case Merge History
Yes — by reactivating Case 2 and removing it from Case 1's related cases
mcq.explanation: Case merges in D365 Customer Service are irreversible. Once Case 2 is merged into Case 1, Case 2 permanently has Canceled — Merged status and cannot be un-merged. There is no undo function for merges. This makes it important to confirm the correct primary case before proceeding.

wrongExplanations.0: There is no Undo Merge button — merges are permanent.
wrongExplanations.2: There is no Case Merge History feature in Settings that allows reversal.
wrongExplanations.3: Reactivating the secondary case and removing it from related records does not truly undo the merge — the activities have been moved and the merged status cannot be reversed.

mcq.q: Which of the following is NOT a supported source type for Automatic Record Creation and Update Rules in D365 Customer Service?
options: Email
Social Activity
Custom Activity
Power BI Alert
mcq.explanation: The supported source types (activity types) for Automatic Record Creation and Update Rules include Email, Social Activity, and Custom Activity types (when configured). Power BI Alerts are not a supported source type — they are a reporting and alerting tool external to the D365 activity framework.

wrongExplanations.0: Email is a core source type — inbound emails are the most common trigger for auto case creation.
wrongExplanations.1: Social Activity is a supported source type for creating cases from social media interactions.
wrongExplanations.2: Custom Activities can be used as source types when they are configured as activity entities.

mcq.q: The four steps to setting up entity record channels and record routing for Automatic Record Creation rules are completed in which order?
options: Create rule → Define conditions → Set actions → Activate
Configure channel → Create rule → Map fields → Test and activate
Enable feature → Define source → Create rule conditions → Publish
Create workstream → Create rule → Define conditions → Activate
mcq.explanation: The four steps for setting up Automatic Record Creation rules follow the sequence: Create the rule (specify source activity type and owner), Define conditions (criteria that must be met for the rule to fire), Set actions (what records to create or update and how to map fields), then Activate the rule. The rule only processes records after activation.

wrongExplanations.1: 'Configure channel' is not the first step — creating the rule is. Field mapping is part of setting actions.
wrongExplanations.2: 'Enable feature' and 'Define source' are not named steps in the documented workflow.
wrongExplanations.3: A workstream is for unified routing, not for Automatic Record Creation rules — the sequence starts with creating the rule.

mcq.q: An agent creates a Phone Call activity for an existing case. When they save it, the Subject field is blank. What is the consequence in D365 Customer Service?
options: The activity is saved and appears in the timeline with 'Unknown Subject'
The save fails — Subject is a required field for Phone Call activities
The activity is saved but will not appear in the case timeline without a Subject
The system auto-fills Subject with the Case Title
mcq.explanation: Subject is a mandatory field for Phone Call activities in D365 Customer Service. Saving a Phone Call without a Subject will fail with a validation error. This applies to all standard activity types — Subject is the required field that identifies the activity purpose and ensures it appears correctly in the timeline.

wrongExplanations.0: The system does not accept 'Unknown Subject' as a substitute — Subject must be explicitly provided.
wrongExplanations.2: The save itself fails — the activity cannot be created without Subject regardless of timeline appearance.
wrongExplanations.3: The system does not auto-fill Subject from the Case Title — the agent must enter it manually.

mcq.q: An agent creates a Task activity but forgets to set the Regarding field to a specific case. Where will this Task appear?
options: It will not appear anywhere — Regarding is mandatory for activities
It appears in the agent's personal Activities view but NOT on any case timeline
It appears on all open cases belonging to the same customer
It appears in the queue's shared Activities view
mcq.explanation: The Regarding field links an activity to a specific record (case, account, contact, etc.). If Regarding is not set, the activity exists in the system but is not linked to any case. It will appear in the agent's own Activities views (My Activities) but will NOT appear on any case timeline. Setting Regarding is essential for the activity to be visible in a case's timeline.

wrongExplanations.0: Regarding is not a mandatory field for activity creation — the activity can be saved without it.
wrongExplanations.2: Activities do not attach to all cases for a customer — they only appear on the specific case set in the Regarding field.
wrongExplanations.3: Unlinked activities do not appear in queue shared views — they appear in personal activity views only.

mcq.q: For an Email activity to appear in a case's Timeline control, which field on the Email must be correctly set?
options: To and From fields must match the case customer
Regarding field must be set to the specific case
Category must be set to 'Case Email'
The Email must be sent — draft emails don't appear in timeline
mcq.explanation: The Regarding field on an Email activity must be set to the specific case for it to appear on that case's Timeline. This is the universal rule for all activity types — the Regarding lookup creates the relationship between the activity and the case record. Without it, the email won't appear in the case timeline regardless of who sent or received it.

wrongExplanations.0: The customer on To/From fields does not automatically associate the email with a case — only the Regarding field does.
wrongExplanations.2: There is no 'Category = Case Email' requirement — the Regarding field is the linking mechanism.
wrongExplanations.3: Draft emails can appear in the timeline if linked via Regarding — but the Regarding field is the primary requirement.

mcq.q: What technology does the advanced similarity rules feature use to find similar cases in D365 Customer Service?
options: Azure Cognitive Search
Relevance Search (Dataverse search)
Quick Find views
SSRS reports with duplicate detection
mcq.explanation: Advanced similarity rules use the Relevance Search (Dataverse search) mechanism. Rules are configured with Text Match or Exact Match criteria on case fields. When enabled, Dataverse search is recommended for most accurate results — without it, the search falls back to primary field matching only.

wrongExplanations.0: Azure Cognitive Search is a separate Azure service — the similarity rules use Dataverse's Relevance Search.
wrongExplanations.2: Quick Find views are for manual search only — similarity rules use Relevance Search for automated suggestion.
wrongExplanations.3: SSRS reports are for scheduled reporting — not for real-time similar case suggestions.

mcq.q: A Business Rule is needed to detect and flag potential duplicate cases when a new case is created. Is this achievable with Business Rules in D365 Customer Service?
options: Yes — Business Rules can query existing cases and block save if a duplicate is found
No — Business Rules cannot query other records. Duplicate detection must use Advanced Similarity Rules or Duplicate Detection Rules
Yes — Business Rules with server-side scope can run OData queries to check for duplicates
No — duplicate detection requires Power Automate only; no built-in tools exist
mcq.explanation: Business Rules in D365 operate on the current record only — they cannot query or compare against other records in the system. For duplicate case detection, the correct tools are Advanced Similarity Rules (for surfacing similar cases to agents) or Dataverse Duplicate Detection Rules (for system-level duplicate flagging). Business Rules simply cannot access data from other records.

wrongExplanations.0: Business Rules cannot query other records — they operate only on the fields of the current form/record.
wrongExplanations.2: Server-side Business Rules still operate on the current record only — they do not execute OData queries.
wrongExplanations.3: Built-in tools do exist — Advanced Similarity Rules and Duplicate Detection Rules are the correct mechanisms.

mcq.q: When configuring an Advanced Similarity Rule, what does setting 'Filter Result by Status = Active' do?
options: Only active cases (where the similar rule itself is active) are used for matching
The rule only returns similar cases that currently have Active status
The search is filtered to only match fields that are active/enabled in the data model
The rule only runs when the current case has Active status
mcq.explanation: The 'Filter Result by Status' setting on an Advanced Similarity Rule controls the status of the suggested similar cases returned. If set to Active, only currently active (open) cases are suggested. If left unset, similar cases in all statuses (active, resolved, canceled) are returned. This helps agents find relevant open cases vs. closed historical ones.

wrongExplanations.0: 'Active' here refers to the status of the target (similar) cases returned — not the rule's own activation state.
wrongExplanations.2: The filter is on case status, not on data model field activation.
wrongExplanations.3: The filter controls what cases are returned in results — not when the rule executes.

mcq.q: Where does an agent view similar case suggestions in the Customer Service Hub?
options: The Case Dashboard > Similar Cases stream
The Related section of the case form > Similar Cases tab
The Knowledge Base tab on the case form
The Activity Timeline filtered to Similar Cases
mcq.explanation: Similar case suggestions are viewed in the Related section of the case form in Customer Service Hub. The agent selects the Similar Cases option in the Related section. Note: the Similar Cases action is not available in the Business Process Flow — it must be accessed via the Related section.

wrongExplanations.0: There is no 'Similar Cases stream' on the Case Dashboard — similar cases are on the case form's Related section.
wrongExplanations.2: The Knowledge Base tab shows knowledge articles — not similar case suggestions.
wrongExplanations.3: The Timeline shows activities linked to the case — not similar case suggestions.

mcq.q: An agent shares a case with a colleague in D365 Customer Service. What does sharing do in terms of case responsibility?
options: Sharing transfers ownership — the colleague becomes the new case owner
Sharing grants the colleague access to view or edit the case without changing the original owner
Sharing routes the case to the colleague's queue for assignment
Sharing creates a copy of the case for the colleague to work on independently
mcq.explanation: Sharing in Dynamics 365 grants another user or team specific access (Read, Write, Delete, Assign, Share, Append, Append To) to a specific record without changing the record's owner. The original owner retains ownership and responsibility. Sharing extends access — it is different from Assign, which transfers ownership.

wrongExplanations.0: Sharing does not change the owner — that is what Assign does.
wrongExplanations.2: Routing to a queue is done via queue membership and routing rules — not by sharing.
wrongExplanations.3: Sharing does not create a copy — both users access the same record.

mcq.q: What is the difference between Dataverse Search (formerly Relevance Search) and Quick Find in D365 Customer Service?
options: Quick Find is faster; Dataverse Search is more accurate
Dataverse Search searches across multiple entities and uses full-text indexing for comprehensive results; Quick Find searches within a single entity using configured search columns
Dataverse Search is only available to admins; Quick Find is available to all users
They are the same — Dataverse Search is just the new name for Quick Find
mcq.explanation: Dataverse Search (formerly Relevance Search) uses Azure Cognitive Service full-text indexing to search across multiple entities simultaneously, returning ranked and comprehensive results. Quick Find searches within a single entity using the configured Quick Find view columns and search fields. Dataverse Search is far more powerful for cross-entity and partial-word searches.

wrongExplanations.0: Speed is not the distinguishing factor — the key difference is scope (cross-entity vs. single entity) and search technology.
wrongExplanations.2: Both search types are available to all users — Dataverse Search is not admin-only.
wrongExplanations.3: They are technically distinct features — Dataverse Search uses Azure indexing; Quick Find uses database query on configured columns.

mcq.q: A manager needs a view that shows all cases assigned to a specific team, regardless of which team member owns each case. Which view type is most appropriate?
options: Personal view — shared with team members
System view (public) — configured by admin to filter by team ownership
Quick view — embedded in the Case form
Lookup view — used to select records from a lookup field
mcq.explanation: A System view (also called a public view) is configured by an administrator and is available to all users with access. It can be set to filter cases by team ownership — for example, 'Team = Customer Support Team'. Personal views are created by individual users for their own use. Quick views are read-only embedded views inside forms. Lookup views appear in lookup field dropdowns.

wrongExplanations.0: Personal views are user-created and not consistently available across the team.
wrongExplanations.2: Quick views are embedded read-only panels on forms — they display related record data, not a filterable case list.
wrongExplanations.3: Lookup views are for selecting records in lookup fields — not for browsing and filtering case lists.

mcq.q: An agent needs to find all cases created by a specific customer in the last 30 days, containing the word 'billing' anywhere in the case title or description. Which search method is most effective?
options: Quick Find on the Cases grid — enter 'billing' in the search box
Dataverse Search — searches across all indexed fields including description with full-text capabilities
Advanced Find — create a filter for customer + created date + title contains 'billing'
Global Filter on the Cases dashboard — set customer and keyword filters
mcq.explanation: Dataverse Search is the most effective tool here because it uses full-text indexing across all searchable fields including description — Quick Find typically only searches the configured Quick Find view columns and may miss description. Advanced Find would also work but requires more manual setup. Dataverse Search gives comprehensive cross-field results with relevance ranking.

wrongExplanations.0: Quick Find only searches fields configured in the Quick Find view — it may not search the description field unless specifically added.
wrongExplanations.2: Advanced Find is a valid option but more manual — Dataverse Search is faster and more comprehensive for keyword searches.
wrongExplanations.3: Global Filter on the dashboard applies to dashboard streams only — it is not a dedicated search tool.

mcq.q: What is the purpose of the Subject Tree in D365 Customer Service case management?
options: It controls which email subjects are monitored for auto case creation
It provides a hierarchical classification system for categorising cases, knowledge articles, and products
It defines the BPF stages for case resolution
It creates a parent-child hierarchy between cases automatically
mcq.explanation: The Subject Tree is a hierarchical classification system used to categorise cases, knowledge articles, and products. Subjects help agents classify the nature of a case (e.g. Billing > Invoice Query > Incorrect Amount) and enable search and filtering by subject. The subject tree is configured by administrators in Case Settings.

wrongExplanations.0: Email subjects for auto case creation are configured via Automatic Record Creation rules — not the Subject Tree.
wrongExplanations.2: BPF stages are configured in the Business Process Flow designer — not the Subject Tree.
wrongExplanations.3: The Subject Tree does not create parent-child relationships between cases — parent-child case settings do.

mcq.q: The Enhanced case experience in D365 Customer Service is available in which app(s)?
options: Customer Service Hub only
Copilot Service workspace only
Both Customer Service Hub and Copilot Service workspace
All model-driven apps with the Case table enabled
mcq.explanation: The Enhanced case creation experience (enhanced full case form and quick case form) is available only in the Copilot Service workspace app. Customer Service Hub does not support the enhanced case form. This distinction is explicitly documented by Microsoft.

wrongExplanations.0: Customer Service Hub does not support the enhanced case experience — it is Copilot Service workspace only.
wrongExplanations.2: The enhanced experience is workspace-only, not both apps.
wrongExplanations.3: The feature is specific to Copilot Service workspace — not all model-driven apps.

mcq.q: What does the Queue Item Control component display on the enhanced case form?
options: All queues the case is currently assigned to and their SLA timers
The current queue the record belongs to and the Worked By field
The full queue history showing every queue the case has been in
The queue routing rules that applied to the case at creation
mcq.explanation: The Queue Item Control component displays the current queue a record belongs to and the Worked By field. The Worked By field can be edited and saved directly from the component. It is available for queue-enabled entities and requires a value in the Table Column property.

wrongExplanations.0: The Queue Item Control shows the current queue only — not SLA timers (those are on the SLA KPI component).
wrongExplanations.2: Queue history is not displayed by this component — it shows the current queue only.
wrongExplanations.3: Routing rules are system configuration — they are not displayed on the case form via this component.

mcq.q: What is the purpose of the OptionSet Wrapper component on the enhanced case form?
options: It creates a dropdown for all option sets in the system
It displays the selected option set values as color-coded options on the form, such as priority icons or status options
It wraps the Status and Status Reason fields into a single combined control
It allows agents to select multiple option set values simultaneously
mcq.explanation: The OptionSet Wrapper component displays option set field values as color-coded visual options on the form. For example, adding the Priority field shows color-coded priority icons. Adding Case Status shows color-coded status options. It is available for choices/option set field types but not for non-editable fields or fields that don't support option set types.

wrongExplanations.0: It does not expose all option sets — it displays a specific configured option set field as a visual, color-coded control.
wrongExplanations.2: It does not combine Status and Status Reason — it is applied to a single option set field.
wrongExplanations.3: Option sets are single-select by nature — the wrapper makes them visual, not multi-select.

mcq.q: The Due Open Activities control is added to a case form. What does it display?
options: All activities ever created for the case regardless of date
Activities that are due today or are overdue
Activities that are in Draft status and not yet saved
Activities linked to the case's SLA KPI milestones
mcq.explanation: The Due Open Activities control specifically shows activities that are due today or are overdue, giving agents an immediate view of urgent pending work. The Table must be set to Activities in the component configuration. It is available for all entities.

wrongExplanations.0: The component filters to due/overdue activities only — not all historical activities on the case.
wrongExplanations.2: Draft status activities are not a specific filter in this component — it filters by due date.
wrongExplanations.3: SLA KPI milestones are tracked separately — this component is about activity due dates.

mcq.q: A case form needs to display the attachments uploaded directly, through email, notes, and conversations. Which component achieves this?
options: Associated Grid Control — set to Attachments entity
Attachment Control — set Table Column to Pre Create Entity Attachment Id for Case
Notes Control — enables rich text note-taking with attachment viewing
Quick View form — embedded Note form showing attachments
mcq.explanation: The Attachment Control is the component that enables uploading and displaying attachments from multiple sources — including direct uploads, email, notes, and conversations. For the Case entity specifically, the Table Column property must be set to 'Pre Create Entity Attachment Id'. This is distinct from the Notes Control, which is for text notes only.

wrongExplanations.0: The Associated Grid Control displays linked related entity records as subgrids — not multi-source attachments.
wrongExplanations.2: The Notes Control allows rich text note-taking — it is for note content, not the full attachment aggregation across emails and conversations.
wrongExplanations.3: A Quick View form embeds another record's form — it doesn't aggregate attachments from multiple activity sources.

dragdrop.instruction: Match each Dynamics 365 Customer Service component to its primary purpose.
zones: Defines time targets for case response and resolution
Defines support terms a customer is entitled to (cases or hours)
Automatically assigns cases to queues based on conditions
Provides hierarchical classification for cases and articles
Stores reusable solutions agents can link to cases

correctMapping.SLA: Defines time targets for case response and resolution
correctMapping.Entitlement: Defines support terms a customer is entitled to (cases or hours)
correctMapping.Routing Rule Set: Automatically assigns cases to queues based on conditions
correctMapping.Subject Tree: Provides hierarchical classification for cases and articles
correctMapping.Knowledge Article: Stores reusable solutions agents can link to cases

dragdrop.explanation: Each component plays a distinct role: SLAs enforce time targets; Entitlements define support contracts; Routing Rules manage queue assignment; Subject Trees classify content; Knowledge Articles capture solutions.

dragdrop.instruction: Match each case status and status reason to the correct lifecycle stage.
zones: Case is open and being worked on
Case has been completed or answered
Case was closed without resolution

correctMapping.Active / In Progress: Case is open and being worked on
correctMapping.Active / On Hold: Case is open and being worked on
correctMapping.Resolved / Problem Solved: Case has been completed or answered
correctMapping.Resolved / Information Provided: Case has been completed or answered

dragdrop.explanation: Active cases (In Progress, On Hold) are still being worked. Resolved cases (Problem Solved, Information Provided) have been answered or fixed. Cancelled/Duplicate cases were closed without a true resolution.

dragdrop.instruction: Classify each configuration action to the correct admin area where it is performed.
items: Add a subject to the Subject Tree
Set up a Record Creation Rule
zones: Customer Service admin centre
Power Platform admin centre or Security settings
Power Apps form editor (make.powerapps.com)

correctMapping.Create a new security role: Power Platform admin centre or Security settings

dragdrop.explanation: Customer Service settings live in the CS admin centre. Security roles are managed in the Power Platform admin centre. Timeline card forms are customised through the Power Apps form editor.

dragdrop.instruction: Match each Case Status to all of its valid out-of-box Status Reason values.
zones: Active status reasons
Resolved status reasons
Canceled status reasons
dragdrop.explanation: The Case table has three Status values each with specific out-of-box Status Reasons. Active has four: In Progress, On Hold, Waiting for Details, and Researching. Resolved has two: Problem Solved and Information Provided. Canceled has two: Canceled (standard cancellation) and Merged (case was merged into another case). Custom Status Reasons can be added per organisation need.

dragdrop.instruction: Arrange the steps in the correct order to configure a customisable case resolution dialog with a custom 'Duplicate' resolution type.
items: In Copilot Service admin center > Case Settings > Other Settings, select Customizable dialog
In Power Apps > Tables > Case > Columns > Status Reason — add 'Duplicate' under Resolved status
Copy the numeric value from the new Case Status Reason choice
In Power Apps > Tables > Case Resolution > Columns > Resolution Type — add 'Duplicate' with the SAME numeric value
Test by resolving a case and confirming 'Duplicate' appears in the Resolution Type dropdown
zones: Step 1
Step 2
Step 3
Step 4
Step 5
Step 6
dragdrop.explanation: The key requirement is that the numeric values for the custom Status Reason (Case table) and Resolution Type (Case Resolution table) must match exactly. Selecting Customizable dialog first enables this customisation path. Adding to Case Status Reason first, copying the value, then adding the matching value to Case Resolution ensures synchronisation. A mismatch will prevent the value from appearing and may cause errors.

dragdrop.instruction: Match each dashboard filter type to how it works and what it affects.
zones: Interactive chart (pie/bar/doughnut) that filters all streams when a segment is clicked — shows at-a-glance metrics and drives drill-down
Field-level criteria applied in a flyout panel — affects all streams, charts, and tiles simultaneously as AND conditions
Date range filter (e.g. This Quarter, Last Quarter, Custom range) that limits which records appear based on creation date
Changes the order of records within a single stream — does not hide or filter records

correctMapping.Visual Filter: Interactive chart (pie/bar/doughnut) that filters all streams when a segment is clicked — shows at-a-glance metrics and drives drill-down
correctMapping.Global Filter: Field-level criteria applied in a flyout panel — affects all streams, charts, and tiles simultaneously as AND conditions
correctMapping.Timeframe Filter: Date range filter (e.g. This Quarter, Last Quarter, Custom range) that limits which records appear based on creation date
correctMapping.Stream Sort: Changes the order of records within a single stream — does not hide or filter records

dragdrop.explanation: Dashboard filtering has four distinct mechanisms. Visual Filters are clickable charts that immediately filter streams — ideal for priority or status breakdowns. Global Filters apply field-level criteria (e.g. Escalated=Yes) across the whole dashboard. Timeframe Filters restrict by date range. Stream Sort changes display order within a stream but does not filter data. Visual and Global filters can be combined as AND conditions.

dragdrop.instruction: Match each case scenario to the most appropriate action an agent should take.
items: Two cases exist for the same customer's same issue — one from email, one from phone
A customer calls back about an issue that was resolved 5 days ago
An agent is going on leave and needs to hand off full case responsibility
A case involves both a billing query and a technical fault — each need separate team work
An agent is out of office temporarily — a colleague needs read access to monitor the case
A case needs to be escalated to Tier 2 and removed from the Tier 1 agent's ownership
zones: Merge the duplicate cases — keep the more complete case as primary
Reactivate the resolved case and continue working on it
Assign the case to the colleague — transferring ownership
Create child cases under the parent for each team to work independently
Share the case with the colleague — granting Read (and optionally Write) access
Reassign the case to the Tier 2 agent or queue

correctMapping.Two cases exist for the same customer's: Merge the duplicate cases — keep the more complete case as primary
correctMapping.A customer calls back about an issue tha: Reactivate the resolved case and continue working on it
correctMapping.An agent is going on leave and needs to: Assign the case to the colleague — transferring ownership
correctMapping.A case involves both a billing query and: Create child cases under the parent for each team to work independently
correctMapping.An agent is out of office temporarily —: Share the case with the colleague — granting Read (and optionally Write) access
correctMapping.A case needs to be escalated to Tier 2 a: Reassign the case to the Tier 2 agent or queue

dragdrop.explanation: Each scenario maps to a specific D365 case action: Merge for true duplicates. Reactivate for returning issues on resolved cases. Assign for full ownership transfer. Child cases for splitting complex multi-team issues. Share for read access without ownership change. Reassign for escalation. Understanding when to use each action prevents data loss, duplication, and SLA confusion.

dragdrop.instruction: Match each parent-child case closure setting to the business scenario it supports.
items: Close all child cases when parent case is closed
Don't allow parent case closure until all child cases are closed
zones: A support organisation has a policy where when the main issue is resolved, all related sub-tasks can be batch-closed at the same time — e.g. closing all departmental sub-tasks when a main IT outage case is resolved
A warranty claims organisation needs to ensure every sub-fault (electrical, mechanical, cosmetic) is individually resolved and documented before the main warranty claim can be closed

correctMapping.Close all child cases when parent case i: A support organisation has a policy where when the main issue is resolved, all related sub-tasks can be batch-closed at the same time — e.g. closing all departmental sub-tasks when a main IT outage case is resolved
correctMapping.Don't allow parent case closure until al: A warranty claims organisation needs to ensure every sub-fault (electrical, mechanical, cosmetic) is individually resolved and documented before the main warranty claim can be closed

dragdrop.explanation: The two closure settings serve opposite business needs. 'Close all children when parent closes' is appropriate when child cases are sub-tasks that naturally complete when the main issue resolves — it prevents orphaned open tasks. 'Don't allow parent closure until children are closed' enforces complete individual resolution of each child before the parent can be marked done — critical for compliance and quality-assurance workflows.

dragdrop.instruction: Arrange the correct sequence of steps to enable Advanced Similarity Rules for similar case suggestions in D365 Customer Service.
items: In Copilot Service admin center > Case Settings, go to Advanced similarity rules > Manage
Select New to create an advanced similarity rule
Set Source Entity to Case and configure Noise Key Phrases and Filter Result by Status
Save the rule, then on the Match Fields tab add Text Analytics Entity Mapping records
Configure Criteria (Text match or Exact match), Entity, and Field for each mapping
Select Activate from the command bar to enable the rule
zones: Step 1
Step 2
Step 3
Step 4
Step 5
Step 6
dragdrop.explanation: Advanced similarity rules follow a create-configure-activate lifecycle. The rule must be saved before match fields can be added. Match fields are where the actual search criteria are defined — Text match for keyword/phrase matching and Exact match for structured field equality. Activation must be the final step — rules only process cases after being activated. Relevance Search should also be enabled for best results.

dragdrop.instruction: Classify each item as something that happens to the Primary Case or the Secondary (Merged) Case during a case merge.
items: Retains its original status (Active) and continues normal processing
All activities from the other case are moved here
Becomes read-only and cannot be updated
All activities are transferred away to the other case
zones: Primary Case (the case that survives the merge)
Secondary Case (the case that is merged/absorbed)

correctMapping.Status becomes Canceled — Merged: Secondary Case (the case that is merged/absorbed)
correctMapping.Retains its original status (Active) and: Primary Case (the case that survives the merge)
correctMapping.All activities from the other case are m: Primary Case (the case that survives the merge)
correctMapping.SLA KPI continues running unaffected: Primary Case (the case that survives the merge)
correctMapping.Becomes read-only and cannot be updated: Secondary Case (the case that is merged/absorbed)
correctMapping.All activities are transferred away to t: Secondary Case (the case that is merged/absorbed)

dragdrop.explanation: In a merge: the Primary Case absorbs all activities from the secondary and continues with its status unchanged (Active), SLA KPI intact, and full editability. The Secondary Case is set to Canceled — Merged, becomes effectively read-only, and loses all its activities to the primary. Merges are irreversible — no undo mechanism exists.

dragdrop.instruction: Match each Automatic Record Creation rule source activity type to the most appropriate scenario for its use.
zones: Inbound customer support emails arriving in a monitored mailbox automatically create cases
Customer complaints or queries posted on Twitter/Facebook automatically create cases for social media monitoring
A custom integration sends IoT device fault alerts through a bespoke activity type that triggers case creation
Service bookings or field service appointments trigger case creation or update rules

correctMapping.Email: Inbound customer support emails arriving in a monitored mailbox automatically create cases
correctMapping.Social Activity: Customer complaints or queries posted on Twitter/Facebook automatically create cases for social media monitoring
correctMapping.Custom Activity: A custom integration sends IoT device fault alerts through a bespoke activity type that triggers case creation
correctMapping.Service Activity: Service bookings or field service appointments trigger case creation or update rules

dragdrop.explanation: Each source type serves a distinct channel: Email is the most common source for inbound case creation from monitored mailboxes. Social Activity sources handle social media channels (Twitter, Facebook) for social listening. Custom Activity types allow organisations to define bespoke activity entities (e.g. IoT alerts, portal submissions) that trigger rules. Service Activity connects field service and appointment scenarios to case management.

dragdrop.instruction: Match each enhanced case form component to its correct configuration requirement.
zones: Table Column must be set to 'Pre Create Entity Attachment Id'
A value must be specified in Table Column; available for queue-enabled entities only
Table must be set to 'Activities'
Specify the required option set in Table Column; not available for non-editable fields
Configure up to four subgrids of linked related entity records

correctMapping.Attachment Control (on Case entity): Table Column must be set to 'Pre Create Entity Attachment Id'
correctMapping.Queue Item Control: A value must be specified in Table Column; available for queue-enabled entities only
correctMapping.OptionSet Wrapper: Specify the required option set in Table Column; not available for non-editable fields
correctMapping.Associated Grid Control: Configure up to four subgrids of linked related entity records

dragdrop.explanation: Each enhanced case form component has specific configuration requirements. Attachment Control needs 'Pre Create Entity Attachment Id' as the table column for case entity. Queue Item Control needs a table column value and is queue-entity-specific. Due Open Activities needs Table=Activities. OptionSet Wrapper needs the specific option set column specified and cannot wrap non-editable fields. Associated Grid Control supports up to four configurable subgrids.

casestudy.title: Contoso Support Centre Setup
casestudy.scenario: Contoso has recently licensed Dynamics 365 Customer Service and is setting up the system from scratch. The support centre has the following requirements: 1. All inbound emails to support@contoso.com must automatically create cases 2. Cases must be categorised using a standard product taxonomy 3. Agents should only be able to edit cases they personally own 4. Supervisors must be able to view and reassign all cases across the team 5. Cases must be resolved within 4 hours of creation, with a warning at 3 hours 6. Agents must confirm the customer was notified before resolving a case The admin team is configuring all of these requirements before going live.

questions.q: Agents must confirm the customer was notified before resolving a case. What is the best configuration approach?
options: Add a mandatory note to the Timeline before resolution is allowed
Use a Business Process Flow with a required Customer Notified field in the stage before Resolved
Configure the Case Resolution dialog to require a confirmation checkbox
Create a Power Automate flow that blocks resolution without a phone call activity
questions.explanation: A Business Process Flow with a required Customer Notified field in the final active stage ensures agents cannot advance to Resolved without confirming customer notification.

wrongExplanations.0: The Timeline does not have a built-in mechanism to block case resolution based on note presence.
wrongExplanations.2: Incorrect — see explanation.
wrongExplanations.3: The Case Resolution dialog can require a description but does not support custom confirmation checkboxes.

questions.q: The organisation wants to prevent agents from resolving a parent case while any child cases are still open. What specific setting achieves this?
options: Set Maximum number of child cases to 0
Select 'Don't allow parent case closure until all child cases are closed' in parent-child case settings
Add a Business Rule on the Case form that checks child case count before save
Configure a workflow to cancel child cases automatically when parent is resolved
questions.explanation: The 'Don't allow parent case closure until all child cases are closed' closure preference setting directly enforces this requirement. It is configured in Copilot Service admin center > Case Settings > Parent child case settings. No custom Business Rules or workflows are needed — this is a built-in setting.

wrongExplanations.0: Setting maximum child cases to 0 would prevent child case creation entirely — not restrict parent closure.
wrongExplanations.2: Business Rules on the Case form operate on individual records only and cannot count related child records in this way.
