wrongExplanations.3: Automatically canceling children would close them without resolution — not the same as requiring them to be properly resolved.

questions.q: After enabling the Customizable dialog for case resolution, Contoso needs the Billable Time field to auto-calculate. What must they ensure is also on the Case Resolution form?
options: The SLA KPI field must be linked on the form
The Total Time field must also be added to the case resolution form
The case handling time widget must be enabled in Experience Profiles
The Regarding field must be added to the Case Resolution form
questions.explanation: Microsoft explicitly documents that the Total Time field must be added to the case resolution dialog for the Billable Time field to display its auto-calculated value. Without Total Time present, Billable Time cannot determine the worked time. Additionally, the Regarding field should NOT be added to the Case Resolution dialog as this is unsupported.

wrongExplanations.0: SLA KPI does not drive Billable Time calculation — Total Time does.
wrongExplanations.2: Case handling time widget (Experience Profiles) is for the case form, not the resolution dialog.
wrongExplanations.3: Adding Regarding to the Case Resolution dialog is explicitly unsupported and will break case resolution.

questions.q: Contoso needs agents to see similar case suggestions when working on a case. Which admin area holds the configuration for this feature?
options: Settings > Duplicate Detection Rules
Copilot Service admin center > Case Settings > Advanced similarity rules > Manage
Power Apps > Tables > Case > Business Rules
Copilot Service admin center > Routing > Classification Rules
questions.explanation: Advanced similarity rules are configured in Copilot Service admin center under Case Settings > Advanced similarity rules > Manage. From there, admins create rules that define which case fields are used for similarity matching (Text match or Exact match). Relevance Search should also be enabled for best results.

wrongExplanations.0: Duplicate Detection Rules detect true duplicates on save — they don't surface similar case suggestions in the Related section.
wrongExplanations.2: Business Rules on the Case table cannot query other records for similarity — they operate on the current record only.
wrongExplanations.3: Classification Rules are part of unified routing for intelligent case routing — not similar case suggestion.

questions.q: Contoso wants to update case description fields on resolved cases for GDPR compliance without reopening them. What is the supported approach?
options: Reactivate every case, update the field, then re-resolve
Use Power Automate flows or API to update fields on resolved cases
Use the Case form command bar's 'Edit Resolved Case' button
This is not possible — resolved cases are permanently read-only
questions.explanation: D365 Customer Service supports updating resolved and canceled cases via Power Automate flows or API without reopening them. This is the documented approach for GDPR compliance scenarios. The feature is enabled by default — configured under Case Settings > Other Settings > Case update after resolution.

wrongExplanations.0: Reactivating is not required — the platform supports direct updates on resolved cases via flow/API.
wrongExplanations.2: There is no 'Edit Resolved Case' button in the command bar — updates must be done via Power Automate or API.
wrongExplanations.3: Resolved cases can be updated via Power Automate/API — they are not permanently read-only.

casestudy.title: Northgate Insurance — Case Routing and Merge Strategy

scenario.title: Northgate Insurance — Case Operations Challenge
scenario.background:
Northgate Insurance runs a customer service centre handling claims (technical), policy queries (billing), and complaint cases. Cases come in via email and social media. The team experiences these problems:

P1. Duplicate cases regularly appear when customers email AND call about the same issue.
P2. Complaint cases require sub-tasks for HR, Legal, and Claims — but currently all work is done on one case.
P3. Agents can currently close a complaint case even while HR and Legal sub-tasks remain open.
P4. The admin team needs to flag resolved cases with a custom 'Withdrawn by Customer' resolution reason.
P5. The management team wants a dashboard that instantly shows claims by priority.
P6. Agents cannot currently see suggested similar cases when working on claim cases.
requirements: P1: Reduce duplicate cases from email and phone channels
P2: Separate sub-tasks per team on complaint cases
P3: Prevent complaint case closure while sub-tasks are open
P4: Add 'Withdrawn by Customer' as a custom resolution type
P5: Dashboard showing claims cases split by priority
P6: Enable similar case suggestions for claim cases

questions.q: For P1 — duplicate cases exist from email and phone for the same issue. What is the immediate remediation an agent should take when they spot the duplicates?
options: Resolve both cases with Status Reason 'Duplicate'
Merge the less complete case into the more complete case — keeping the more complete one as primary
Delete the phone call case and keep the email case
Create a parent case and link both duplicates as children
questions.explanation: When duplicate cases are identified, the agent should merge them — keeping the more complete or earlier case as the primary. All activities from the secondary transfer to the primary, and the secondary becomes Canceled — Merged. This creates a single thread and preserves all customer interactions. Merging is irreversible, so the primary case must be chosen carefully.

wrongExplanations.0: Resolving both as 'Duplicate' would close both cases — the actual issue would go unresolved.
wrongExplanations.2: Deleting a case permanently removes its activities and history — this is not the correct approach.
wrongExplanations.3: Parent-child is for structurally related sub-issues — not for combining true duplicates of the same issue.

questions.q: For P2 — complaint cases need HR, Legal, and Claims to work independently on their parts. Which case management feature enables this?
options: Merge all team cases into one complaint case
Create child cases under the complaint as parent — one per team
Use multiple queues with the complaint case routed to each
Create separate cases per team with no relationship between them
questions.explanation: Parent-child cases are the correct structure here. The complaint case becomes the parent. Three child cases are created — one for HR, one for Legal, one for Claims. Each team works on their own child case independently. The parent complaint case ties them all together and enables the correct closure preference to be applied.

wrongExplanations.0: Merging would combine everything into one case — teams cannot work independently after a merge.
wrongExplanations.2: Routing to multiple queues still means one case — teams cannot track their work separately on a single case.
wrongExplanations.3: Separate unlinked cases lose the connection to the parent complaint and make it impossible to enforce closure rules.

questions.q: For P3 — to prevent complaint case closure while HR, Legal, and Claims child cases remain open, which setting should the admin apply?
options: Set Maximum number of child cases to 3
Select 'Don't allow parent case closure until all child cases are closed'
Add a workflow that checks child case status on parent case save
Select 'Close all child cases when parent case is closed'
questions.explanation: 'Don't allow parent case closure until all child cases are closed' is the built-in setting that directly prevents the parent complaint case from being resolved or canceled until all three child cases (HR, Legal, Claims) are individually closed. This enforces the required quality gate without needing custom workflows.

wrongExplanations.0: Setting maximum to 3 limits how many children exist — it does not block parent closure.
wrongExplanations.2: A workflow is possible but unnecessary — the built-in closure preference setting is the correct and simpler tool.
wrongExplanations.3: 'Close all child cases when parent is closed' does the opposite — it batch-closes children when the parent closes.

questions.q: For P4 — to add 'Withdrawn by Customer' as a custom resolution type in the Customizable dialog, which two tables must be updated with matching numeric values?
options: Case table and Activity table
Case table (Status Reason column) and Case Resolution table (Resolution Type column)
Case Resolution table and Queue Item table
Status Reason table and Resolution Type table (separate admin tables)
questions.explanation: To add a custom resolution type to the Customizable dialog: first add 'Withdrawn by Customer' to the Status Reason column on the Case table (under Resolved status), then copy the numeric value, and add the same label with the identical numeric value to the Resolution Type column on the Case Resolution table. If values don't match, the option won't appear and errors may occur.

wrongExplanations.0: The Activity table is not involved in case resolution dialog customisation.
wrongExplanations.2: Queue Item table is not involved in this customisation — the Case and Case Resolution tables are the correct pair.
wrongExplanations.3: There are no separate 'Status Reason table' or 'Resolution Type table' — these are columns on the Case and Case Resolution tables respectively.

questions.q: For P5 — management wants a dashboard showing claim cases split by priority. Which dashboard feature should be configured?
options: Global Filter — set Priority field criteria in the flyout
Visual Filter — enable a Cases by Priority chart on the dashboard
Stream sort — sort all streams by Priority descending
Timeframe Filter — set to This Week to see current priority distribution
questions.explanation: A Visual Filter configured as a 'Cases by Priority' chart (e.g. pie or doughnut chart) gives management an immediate visual breakdown of cases by priority. Clicking any segment filters the streams to show only that priority level. This is the purpose-built feature for priority-based dashboard visualisation — exactly what management needs.

wrongExplanations.0: Global Filter applies AND-criteria filtering but doesn't give a visual breakdown by priority — it would hide all but one priority level at a time.
wrongExplanations.2: Stream sort changes order within streams — it does not provide a visual split or aggregate count by priority.
wrongExplanations.3: Timeframe Filter restricts by date range — it doesn't show priority distribution.

scenario.title: Contoso Telecom — End-to-End Case Configuration
scenario.background:
Contoso Telecom is implementing D365 Customer Service. The admin team has the following configuration tasks:

T1. Ensure inbound emails to support@contoso.com automatically create cases and route to the correct queue.
T2. Allow the GDPR team to update resolved case descriptions via automation without reopening cases.
T3. Ensure agents see related open cases when working on a new case.
T4. The enhanced case form should show color-coded case priority and display which queue the case is in.
T5. The Case Handling Time widget needs to be configured to track active work time for all representatives.

questions.q: For T1 — which feature creates cases automatically from inbound emails to support@contoso.com and routes them to queues?
options: BPF triggered by new email activities
Automatic Record Creation and Update Rules with Email as the source activity type
Unified Routing assignment rules triggered by email queue items
Power Automate flow listening to the mailbox via Exchange connector
questions.explanation: Automatic Record Creation and Update Rules with Email as the source type is the built-in D365 Customer Service feature for this exact scenario. The rule monitors inbound email, checks configured conditions, creates a case, and can route it to the appropriate queue. This is the standard out-of-box automation for email-to-case workflows.

wrongExplanations.0: BPFs define case workflow stages — they do not monitor mailboxes or create cases from emails.
wrongExplanations.2: Unified Routing assigns work items to agents and queues — it operates after case creation, not as the creation mechanism.
wrongExplanations.3: A Power Automate flow is technically possible but is not the standard built-in tool — Automatic Record Creation rules are the correct D365 feature.

questions.q: For T2 — the GDPR team needs to update description fields on resolved cases via automation. What setting must the admin configure and what tool does the GDPR team use?
options: Case Settings > Resolve case dialog > Enable editable resolved cases; GDPR team uses the case form
Case Settings > Other Settings > Allow updates for = Resolved and canceled cases; GDPR team uses Power Automate flows or API
Settings > Security > Give GDPR team Write access on resolved cases; they edit directly from the form
No setting needed — resolved cases are always editable from the case form UI
questions.explanation: The setting is in Case Settings > Other Settings > Case update after resolution > Allow updates for = Resolved and canceled cases. Updates must be performed via Power Automate flows or API — not directly from the case form UI. This allows the GDPR team to automate data redaction on resolved cases without reopening them.

wrongExplanations.0: There is no 'Enable editable resolved cases' toggle in the Resolve case dialog — the setting is in Other Settings.
wrongExplanations.2: Security role Write access alone is not sufficient — resolved cases have application-level restrictions that the Other Settings configuration controls. Also, form-based editing is not supported for resolved cases.
wrongExplanations.3: Resolved cases are not editable from the form UI by default — the Power Automate/API path is required.

questions.q: For T3 — agents need to see related open cases when working on a new case. Which feature and admin configuration achieves this?
options: Create a Quick Find view filtered to related cases and pin it to the agent toolbar
Configure Advanced Similarity Rules in Case Settings and ensure Relevance Search is enabled
Add an Associated Grid Control to the case form linked to Related Cases
Enable the 'Show related cases' toggle in Experience Profiles
questions.explanation: Advanced Similarity Rules configured in Copilot Service admin center (Case Settings > Advanced similarity rules) enable the system to surface similar/related cases in the Related section of the case form. Enabling Relevance Search ensures the most accurate and comprehensive matching. Agents then see similar cases in the Related section without leaving the case form.

wrongExplanations.0: Quick Find views are manual search tools — they don't automatically surface related cases to agents within a case form.
wrongExplanations.2: The Associated Grid Control displays explicitly linked child/related records — not AI-suggested similar cases based on content.
wrongExplanations.3: There is no 'Show related cases' toggle in Experience Profiles — similar cases are driven by Advanced Similarity Rules.

questions.q: For T4 — color-coded priority and current queue display on the enhanced case form. Which two components achieve this?
options: Notes Control (priority) and Associated Grid Control (queue)
OptionSet Wrapper (priority as color-coded icons) and Queue Item Control (current queue and Worked By)
Attachment Control (priority attachment) and Due Open Activities (queue activities)
Associated Grid Control (priority subgrid) and OptionSet Wrapper (queue color coding)
questions.explanation: The OptionSet Wrapper component displays the Priority option set as color-coded icons — this is exactly what it is designed for. The Queue Item Control displays the current queue the case belongs to and the Worked By field, which can be edited directly. These two components together address both requirements in T4.

wrongExplanations.0: Notes Control is for rich text note-taking — it does not display priority. Associated Grid Control shows related record subgrids, not queue membership.
wrongExplanations.2: Attachment Control manages file attachments — not priority. Due Open Activities shows overdue tasks — not queue assignment.
wrongExplanations.3: The Associated Grid Control and OptionSet Wrapper combination is reversed — OptionSet Wrapper handles priority and Queue Item Control handles queue display.

questions.q: For T5 — the Case Handling Time widget must be added to the case form in Customer Service Hub for automatic time tracking. What property must be set on the Handling Time component?
options: Table: Time Trackers; View: All Time Trackers
Table: Case; View: Active Cases
Table: Activities; View: All Activities
Table: Case Resolution; View: All Resolutions
questions.explanation: When adding the Handling Time component to the case form, the component properties must be set to Table: Time Trackers and View: All Time Trackers. This connects the widget to the msdyn_timetracker table where automatic and manual time entries are stored. In Customer Service Hub specifically, this widget must be present on the form for time tracking to work — unlike Copilot Service workspace which tracks in the background regardless.

wrongExplanations.1: Table: Case / View: Active Cases connects to case records, not the time tracking table.
wrongExplanations.2: Activities are a different entity — the time tracker table is msdyn_timetracker (Time Trackers).
wrongExplanations.3: Case Resolution is the resolution details table — not where time tracking records are stored.
